title: stipa pennata subvar. laevis
intro: Stipa pennata subvar. laevis, also known as the meadow millet or rough meadow grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Stipa pennata subvar. laevis are said to include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to create remedies for various health issues.
